skill.name: prettify-pdf
skill.description: Redesign and QA PDFs for readability while preserving IDs, dates, amounts, labels, barcodes, QR codes, seals, signatures, and source meaning.
skill.summary: Improve PDF readability without changing critical document facts or official visual assets.
skill.author: sethupavan12

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical matches, but review found that most are Markdown examples, URLs, regex terms, and release hygiene warnings rather than malicious behavior. The real risk is an optional local PDF helper that parses user-supplied PDFs and writes extracted text, page renders, images, and a manifest to a user-chosen output directory. No evidence found of runtime network calls, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local PDF Parsing and Artifact Extraction
medium_findings.description: The optional helper opens a user-supplied PDF with PyMuPDF, renders pages, extracts embedded images, and writes text, image assets, and a manifest. This is legitimate for the skill, but processing untrusted PDFs and writing extracted document artifacts can expose sensitive local data if users choose unsafe inputs or output locations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly processes a PDF path and writes derived artifacts locally. The behavior matches the documented skill purpose and has no network path, so the concern is controlled local data handling rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Encourages External Install Commands
medium_findings.description: The README and docs include shell commands for cloning repositories and installing packages. These are expected installation examples, but users should understand that running them performs external downloads in their own environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command patterns are real but appear only as user-facing documentation and are not executed by the skill automatically. The risk depends on user choice to run documented setup commands.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings appear to be caused by words such as PDF, QR, IDs, hashes, and regex patterns in documentation and helper code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic primitives used for security decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed representative high-signal files and found preservation guidance and regex extraction logic, not cryptographic operations. The static category does not match the semantic context.

low_findings.title: Static Credential Findings Are Release Hygiene Text
low_findings.description: The reported environment and sensitive-file findings are guidance that forbids committing private files, API keys, tokens, cookies, credentials, or .env files. No evidence found that the skill reads secrets or credential stores.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced code checks for forbidden release paths and the documentation tells maintainers to avoid credentials. This is protective validation, not credential access.

low_findings.title: Static Network Findings Are Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings point to repository badges, homepage metadata, and installation examples. No evidence found of runtime HTTP requests or background network communication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation and metadata. The reviewed Python scripts do not import network libraries or make outbound requests.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Most shell command detections are fenced command examples for installation, validation, or optional helper usage. No evidence found of subprocess, shell=True, eval, exec, or automatic command execution in the reviewed scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The patterns are present as documentation text. The Python helper uses argparse and PyMuPDF APIs rather than shelling out.

content.value_statement: Important PDFs often become easier to read only after risky manual redesign. This skill guides agents to improve layout while preserving exact values, labels, and official assets.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents through source PDF inspection before redesign work starts.
Builds preservation lists for IDs, dates, amounts, names, labels, and operational facts.
Requires exact preservation of QR codes, barcodes, seals, signatures, stamps, and logos.
Supports redesign guidance for receipts, invoices, appointments, forms, reports, and resumes.
Provides an optional local helper script that extracts text, page renders, images, and a manifest.
Defines final QA checks for clipped text, changed labels, missing assets, and layout collisions.
limitations: It does not provide a hosted PDF rendering service or cloud workflow.
OCR quality depends on tools available in the user environment.
Barcode and QR decoding are not guaranteed by the included helper script.
The final result still needs visual review for sensitive or official documents.

use_cases.title: Receipt Redesign
use_cases.description: Create a clearer payment receipt while keeping receipt numbers, bank references, totals, barcodes, and seals unchanged.

use_cases.title: Appointment Confirmation Cleanup
use_cases.description: Make date, time, location, applicant, reference number, and required actions easy to find without changing source facts.

use_cases.title: Invoice QA Review
use_cases.description: Compare a redesigned invoice against the original and flag changed IDs, totals, terms, missing logos, or layout collisions.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign a Simple PDF
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a clearer document layout.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $prettify-pdf to redesign this PDF for readability. Preserve all IDs, dates, amounts, names, labels, and official visual assets exactly.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Receipt
prompt_templates.scenario: Receipt or payment proof with important references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $prettify-pdf to improve this receipt. Highlight the total, receipt number, payment date, payee, and transaction references. Keep the barcode and seal unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Clean Up an Appointment Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $prettify-pdf to make this confirmation easier to scan. Prioritize date, time, location, applicant, reference number, and required documents. Do not relabel ambiguous fields.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Redesigned PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Use $prettify-pdf to compare the redesigned PDF with the original. Report any changed values, missing machine-readable assets, clipped text, label changes, or layout collisions.

output_examples.input: A scanned appointment confirmation with a busy table, QR code, applicant name, appointment window, and application number.
output: A redesigned PDF that places the appointment details in a clear summary area.
A verification note confirming exact preservation of the applicant name, application number, date, time, location, and QR code.
A limitation note if OCR or barcode decoding could not be completed.

output_examples.input: An invoice redesign that needs review before sending to a client.
output: A concise QA report covering invoice number, dates, payment terms, line items, subtotal, tax, and total.
A visual layout decision that identifies whether the PDF is safe to deliver.
A list of any missing logo, missing barcode, clipped text, or unclear amount hierarchy.

best_practices: Create a preservation list before changing layout or typography.
Render the final PDF and inspect the visual result, not only extracted text.
Keep original labels when source meaning is ambiguous.
anti_patterns: Do not recreate QR codes, barcodes, seals, stamps, signatures, or logos.
Do not normalize IDs, timestamps, names, or currency values unless the source already uses that form.
Do not deliver a visually attractive PDF if any critical value is clipped, hidden, or relabeled.

faq.question: Does this skill upload PDFs to a service?
faq.answer: No. The skill is written for local agent workflows and does not require a hosted service or API key.

faq.question: Can it redesign official receipts or forms?
faq.answer: Yes, but it emphasizes exact preservation of facts and official assets before visual improvement.

faq.question: Does it decode QR codes or barcodes?
faq.answer: The skill requires preserving them visually. Decoding depends on tools available in the user environment.

faq.question: What files can the optional helper create?
faq.answer: It can create extracted text, page images, embedded image files, and a manifest in the chosen output directory.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is provider-neutral and includes compatibility guidance for these agent environments.

faq.question: What should users check before delivery?
faq.answer: Users should verify exact values, source labels, machine-readable assets, page renders, and any layout collision.
